common.layoutDescription: Free and ultra-secure online PDF, Word, Excel, and image privacy metadata scanner & one-click cleaner. MetaDocu runs 100% locally in your browser sandbox — your files never leave your device. Deeply strip author names, company names, local file paths, comments, random revision history (RSIDs), and embedded EXIF/GPS photos.

home.enterWorkState: 100% Browser Local Sandbox Running · Zero Network Upload
home.description: Before sharing PDF, Word, Excel, or image files online, deeply scan and clean hidden author names, company info, local development absolute paths, revision histories (RSID), and media EXIF details. Everything runs locally in your browser sandbox.
home.moreComing: We promise to never access your document content. All operations are performed 100% safely within your local browser sandbox.
home.solutionSectionDesc: For common document formats shared and submitted online, MetaDocu provides professional browser-local metadata sanitization solutions, targeting key sensitive properties and rebuilding file binary structures safely.
home.dragUploadStart: Drag & drop files here to audit privacy
home.singleOrBatchDesc: Supports PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, JPG, PNG. Directly drag files in to get a detailed privacy report instantly.
home.seoSectionTitle: The First 100% Browser-Local Metadata Privacy Audit Tool
home.seoSectionDesc: MetaDocu is a professional browser-local metadata privacy scanner and cleanup utility. Unlike standard online metadata editors, MetaDocu does not require uploading any of your files to a remote server. All complex operations, including Office XML tree reconstruction, PDF Xref offset recalculation, RSID paragraph tag stripping, and embedded image EXIF purging, are executed safely in your local browser sandbox.
home.featureWordDesc: Instantly scan and strip creator, manager, company name, and last modified by. Clean random revision histories (RSID) embedded within paragraphs, and recursively strip camera EXIF data from nested media images.
home.featureExcelDesc: Scan PDF files to detect digital signatures (warning triggered) to prevent file hash corruption. Gracefully capture password-protected PDFs for local decryption, and physically rebuild the PDF layout with Xref recalculation.
home.featurePdfDesc: Scan and physically wipe camera metadata, capture timestamp, and geographic GPS coordinates from JPEG/PNG images. Retain perfect image resolution while removing all metadata footprints.

faqItems.q: Are my files uploaded to your server?
faqItems.a: Absolutely not. MetaDocu is 100% browser-local. The metadata scanning and cleaning processes run entirely within your local WebAssembly sandbox. Your files never leave your device.

faqItems.q: What metadata privacy risks can this tool detect?
faqItems.a: It deeply parses and strips over 20 sensitive items, including creator name, last modified by, creation/modification times, local development template absolute paths, software versions, paragraph RSIDs, and image GPS locations.

faqItems.q: How do I check PDF metadata without uploading?
faqItems.a: Simply drag and drop your PDF into the upload zone. The local WebAssembly engine parses the document's Info dictionary in browser memory, listing all properties on your screen with zero network transfer.

faqItems.q: Can Word documents reveal comments or revision history?
faqItems.a: Yes. Word documents record random paragraph RSID markers that trace revision histories and editor footprints. MetaDocu purges these internal XML nodes cleanly, restoring privacy.

Yes. Traditional online metadata utilities require uploading contracts, resumes, or financial spreadsheets to their servers, inviting leakages. MetaDocu performs a strict client-side sandbox cleanup, locking down your data.
Yes. Smart phones and digital cameras record capture parameters and GPS locations into EXIF segments. MetaDocu performs a physical byte-strip of EXIF chunks, outputting clean images.

faqQuestions.edit-word-author: How to edit the author information of a Word document online?
faqQuestions.office-metadata-info: What information is included in Office document metadata?
faqQuestions.edit-pdf-properties: Can PDF file properties be edited?
faqQuestions.batch-clear-metadata: How to batch clear metadata from multiple documents?
faqQuestions.online-metadata-security: Is it safe to edit document metadata online?
faqQuestions.view-word-metadata: Where can I view the metadata of a Word document?
faqQuestions.modify-word-detectable: Can others tell if Word document properties have been modified?
faqQuestions.remove-word-privacy: How to completely delete personal privacy information in a Word document?
faqQuestions.excel-metadata-info: What information is included in Excel workbook metadata?
faqQuestions.pdf-metadata-location: Where is PDF file metadata stored?
faqQuestions.word-to-pdf-metadata: Will metadata be preserved after converting Word to PDF?
faqQuestions.word-hidden-metadata: What hidden metadata is in a Word document?
faqQuestions.delete-properties-visible: Can others still see the properties after deleting them?
faqQuestions.batch-delete-word-privacy: How to batch delete privacy information from multiple Word documents?
faqQuestions.batch-edit-excel-properties: How to batch edit the properties of multiple Excel files?
faqQuestions.excel-formula-impact: Will Excel formulas and charts be affected after modifying properties?
faqQuestions.clear-pdf-hidden-info: How to completely clear all hidden information in a PDF?
faqQuestions.online-pdf-upload: Do I need to upload files to edit PDF properties online?

faqAnswers.edit-word-author: Open the Office Metadata Editor, upload your Word document, locate the author field in the editor interface, modify it, and save. The entire process is done locally in your browser — your document is never uploaded to any server.
faqAnswers.office-metadata-info: Metadata includes author name, creation time, modification time, company name, title, subject, tags, notes, and more. This information may inadvertently leak your personal or company privacy.
faqAnswers.edit-pdf-properties: Yes. Upload a PDF file through the online Office Metadata Editor to edit properties such as title, author, and subject. All processing is done locally in your browser, safe and convenient.
faqAnswers.batch-clear-metadata: Open the Batch Workspace, add multiple files, and use the batch clear feature to remove metadata from all files in one click. Supports mixed batch processing of Word, Excel, and PDF files.
faqAnswers.online-metadata-security: It is very safe. This tool processes everything locally in your browser. Your files are never uploaded to any server — all operations are completed on your device, fully protecting your data privacy.
faqAnswers.view-word-metadata: In Word, go to File → Info → Properties → Advanced Properties to view complete metadata, including author, revision count, editing time, and other hidden data.
faqAnswers.modify-word-detectable: This tool removes or overwrites common visible metadata fields. However, some applications or forensic tools may expose additional hidden information depending on the file format and editing history.
faqAnswers.remove-word-privacy: Upload your Word document to the editor, clear all personal fields (author, company, notes, etc.) in the metadata editing area, save, and download. It is also recommended to check custom properties in the document properties panel.
faqAnswers.excel-metadata-info: Excel workbook metadata includes author, creation date, modification date, company name, title, subject, category, and more. This information travels with the file and may leak sensitive information.
faqAnswers.pdf-metadata-location: PDF metadata is stored in the document information dictionary (Info Dictionary) in the PDF header, including standard fields such as title, author, subject, keywords, creation date, and modification date.
faqAnswers.word-to-pdf-metadata: Possibly. When converting DOCX to PDF, some metadata (such as original creator and company name) may be transferred to the PDF property fields. It is recommended to clear sensitive information before conversion or use the PDF metadata editor.
faqAnswers.word-hidden-metadata: In addition to document properties like author and title, Word documents may also contain revision marks, comment history, hidden text, document version information, printer paths, and other less obvious metadata.
faqAnswers.delete-properties-visible: After thorough removal with this tool, others cannot see the information. The tool removes the relevant fields from the document's internal XML structure rather than simply hiding them in the UI, ensuring the information is permanently deleted.
faqAnswers.batch-delete-word-privacy: Use the Batch Workspace to add multiple Word documents at once, select the batch clear metadata feature, and process all files simultaneously in one click, greatly improving efficiency.
faqAnswers.batch-edit-excel-properties: Through the batch processing feature, import multiple Excel files at once and edit or clear property information uniformly. Supports batch setting of common fields such as author and company.
faqAnswers.excel-formula-impact: No. Metadata editing only affects document property information (such as author, title, etc.) and does not touch the data, formulas, charts, or formatting within the workbook.
faqAnswers.clear-pdf-hidden-info: Upload your PDF file, clear all property fields in the editor, save and download. The tool uses the pdf-lib library to directly manipulate the PDF structure, ensuring information is completely removed.
faqAnswers.online-pdf-upload: No upload to any server is required. This tool runs locally in your browser, using web technologies to process PDF files directly on the client — your files remain on your device at all times.
